institutional.title: About us
institutional.intro: The Entre Ríos Golf Federation is the body that brings together and represents organized golf in the province, connecting clubs, competitions and players under one system.
institutional.objectivesP1: FEG's central mission is to organize, regulate and promote golf in Entre Ríos. This means ensuring fair competition, maintaining the integrity of provincial rankings and representing affiliated clubs before the Argentine Golf Association.
institutional.objectivesP2: We are committed to transparency in tournament and results management, developing the game across all categories — from juniors to senior golf — and strengthening an active, competitive golf community throughout the province.
institutional.pillar01Body: Honest management of rankings, handicaps and results.
institutional.pillar02Body: Formal link with the AAG and national bodies.
institutional.pillar03Body: Promoting golf across all categories and regions.
institutional.regionalP1: Entre Ríos plays an active role in Argentina's federated golf circuit. FEG connects provincial clubs with the national competition system, coordinating the annual tournament calendar, managing regional inter-federation participation and representing Entre Ríos players in selection events.
institutional.regionalP2: Provincial geography — with clubs from Concordia to Gualeguaychú, from Paraná to Colón — requires year-round coordination so no part of the circuit is left out of federated play.
institutional.visionTitle: The golf we want to build
institutional.visionP1: We see Entre Ríos golf as a growing ecosystem: a community of players, clubs and institutions that strengthen one another. Our vision is for Entre Ríos to become a regional golf reference in Argentina, with nationally competitive players and a modern federation that supports that growth.
institutional.visionP2: Long term, we aim to broaden access to the sport — especially among youth — and create conditions for provincial talent to project beyond the province. Well-managed organized sport is also a path to belonging and identity.

president.summary: Leads the federation and sets institutional guidelines.

vicePresident.summary: Supports management and coordinates actions with clubs and committees.

treasury.summary: Manages budgets, payments and financial reporting.

secretary.summary: Organizes minutes, documentation and institutional agenda.

press.summary: Coordinates communication, publications and event coverage.

rankingRules.title: Regulations
rankingRules.subtitle: Argentine Youth and Pre-youth Ranking 2026 — rules, quotas and registration.
rankingRules.heading: Argentine Youth and Pre-youth Ranking 2026
rankingRules.intro: Guidelines and methodology for countable competitions, quotas, allocation criteria and registration.
rankingRules.nationalTournamentsDesc: four 54-hole tournaments with Women's and Men's categories.
rankingRules.generalCategoryDesc: Youth (Juniors) and Pre-youth (M15) jointly. WAGR scoring.
rankingRules.venue1: F.R.G. Sur, Golf Club Sierra de la Ventana
rankingRules.venue2: F.R.G. Mar y Sierras, Costa Esmeralda Golf & Links
rankingRules.venue3: Federation of Southern Littoral, host club TBC
rankingRules.championshipDesc: October 2–4 (Córdoba Province Golf Federation, host club TBD), under its own regulations.
rankingRules.fieldBody: Maximum field of 120 players in each national tournament. A category cut after 36 holes will be implemented to allow 13 starting groups from holes 1 and 10 and facilitate early finish or recovery of pending holes.
rankingRules.quotasBody: Quotas are set according to the final 2025 Argentine Ranking by region, weighting points from national (not regional) tournaments, considering class 2010 (moving to Youth) and excluding class 2007. A minimum quota of 1 is granted when there is no score, and additional Pre-youth quotas are calculated from performances at the 2025 National Junior Tournament. Quotas are unnamed.
rankingRules.quotasNote: Note: details and supplementary notes are in the downloadable PDF.
rankingRules.quotasRecalc: After the 2nd national tournament, quotas will be recalculated based on participation and performance. Once national tournaments finish, men in the top five of their ranking and women in the top two (Youth and Pre-youth) are guaranteed a quota for the next tournament (not additional).
rankingRules.assignmentBody: Federations and the Metropolitan Area define the classification methodology for each national tournament, combining: regional/federated tournament results (or qualifiers), current federation ranking and/or sporting merit. Quotas are nominated by federation and reported to the AAG Championships Commission.
rankingRules.handicapSwapNote: Each federation may, at its discretion, swap one quota (male and female) between youth and pre-youth, formalizing the change by email to AAG Championships.
rankingRules.regionalP1: Regional tournaments will have special scoring for each ranking (scale to be published). Registration is implemented through federations via an online quota assignment form, and each player must also complete an individual registration form before the deadline to be entered.
rankingRules.regionalP2: Registration fees vary depending on accommodation (offered by the host federation/club or not). The club or federation may request advance payment with accommodation, and cancellations within 10 days before the tournament start may be subject to the respective charge.

enrollment.title: Registration {year}
enrollment.intro: Complete the form for junior players interested in FEG activities and competitions in the {year} season. Data is kept confidential in the platform's registered juniors roster.
enrollment.importantNotice: Important: the form must be completed by the player's parents (mother, father and/or guardian) as a sworn declaration. It is an annual process and should only be done once per season. FEG ensures confidential handling.

fields.responsibleName: Name of person submitting the form
fields.birthDateHint: You can select the year by clicking the year selector at the top of the date picker.

enrollment.successBody: Registration was recorded successfully for the {year} season.
enrollment.dniAlreadyEnrolled: This player is already registered for the {year} season. You do not need to complete the form again.

errors.ageOutOfRange: The player's age does not match junior/youth categories for the season.

pwa.iosDialogAria: How to enable notifications on iPhone
pwa.iosBody: Push notifications can only be enabled if you open FEG from the home screen icon, not from Safari. If you have not added it yet: Share → Add to Home Screen, open that app and tap Enable there.
pwa.pushBody: Get alerts when there is new news. Tap or click Enable and accept in the system dialog (not just this bar).

errors.subscribeFailed: Could not subscribe to push service
errors.iosStandaloneRequired: Open FEG from your home screen icon. In Safari alone, permission does not apply to the installed app.
